HomePage.title: The Playground
HomePage.metaTitle: Launch your community, organize your events
HomePage.metaDescription: The free platform to build your community and host events. A free alternative to Meetup and Luma, with no fees or subscriptions.
HomePage.heroSubtitle: The free platform to build your community and host unforgettable events.
HomePage.mockupAboutText: The community for Product enthusiasts in the age of AI.
HomePage.step1Description: Your community space in 30 seconds. Public or private, you decide.
HomePage.step2Description: Every Event gets a premium, shareable page. One link, and you're live.
HomePage.step3Description: Members stay in your community and come back for the next Events.
HomePage.pillar1Description: Every event registration automatically becomes a community membership. Your members stay. Your audience grows with every event.
HomePage.pillar2Description: Export your members, events, and history anytime. No lock-in, no ransom. What you build stays yours.
HomePage.pillar3Description: Free for you, free for your members. No hidden fees, no artificial limits. All features, for every community.
HomePage.audience1Description: You organize meetups, conferences or workshops — tech, product, design, data, or any other field.
HomePage.audience2Description: You run a sports club, cultural association or local support network.
HomePage.audience3Description: You bring together a network of freelancers, entrepreneurs or professionals around a shared field.
HomePage.audience4Description: You run workshops, training sessions or recurring events and want to build a loyal audience.
HomePage.comparisonSubtitle: There are several platforms for organizing events. None combines a persistent community, premium experience and total free access.
HomePage.comparisonText: The Playground is the free platform for community organizers who want more than just an event tool. Unlike Meetup (mandatory organizer subscription) or Luma (isolated events, no retention), The Playground combines a persistent community, premium event pages and total free access — no platform commission, no subscription, no artificial limits.
HomePage.faqA1: Yes, 100% free. No subscription, no platform commission, no limit on the number of communities, events or members. If you host paid events, only standard Stripe fees apply (~2.9% + $0.30 per transaction) — The Playground takes no cut.
HomePage.faqA2: Meetup has a solid community model (groups, members), but requires a paid organizer subscription and offers a dated experience. The Playground takes Meetup's community model — with a modern UX inspired by Luma, premium event pages, and most importantly total free access for organizers.
HomePage.faqA3: Luma excels at the event experience (beautiful pages, smooth registration), but every event is an island — no retention between events. The Playground adds the community layer that Luma is missing: your attendees automatically become members of your community and come back for future events.
HomePage.faqQ4: How do I create a community?
HomePage.faqA4: Create your community in 30 seconds: a name, a description, a theme — that's it. Your community is immediately ready. You can then create events, invite members via link, and customize your community page. No approval, no waiting.
HomePage.faqA5: Yes. Ticketing is built in via Stripe Connect. You receive payments directly to your Stripe account — The Playground takes no commission. Only standard Stripe processing fees apply.
HomePage.faqA6: Yes. You can export your members and registrations as CSV anytime. No lock-in, no tricks. What you build on The Playground stays yours.
HomePage.faqQ7: Is my data stored in Europe?
HomePage.faqA7: Core data — database, app hosting, analytics, monitoring — is hosted in the European Union (Germany). Some subprocessors (emails, payments, AI) operate in the US with EU standard contractual clauses and DPAs in place. No data is sold or shared for advertising purposes.
HomePage.ctaFinalSubtitle: Create your community in 30 seconds. Free, forever.
HomePage.contactTitle: Have a question before getting started?
HomePage.contactSubtitle: We reply within 24h, or check our

editor.title: Publisher
editor.content: The Playground is published by The Spark SASU, a simplified single-shareholder corporation registered in France.
editor.address: Registered office: 10 rue de la Paix, 75002 Paris, France

host.title: Hosting provider
host.content: The website is hosted by Vercel Inc., 340 S Lemon Ave #4133, Walnut, CA 91789, United States.

intellectualProperty.title: Intellectual property
intellectualProperty.content: All content on this website (text, graphics, logos, icons, software) is the exclusive property of The Spark SASU, unless otherwise stated. Any reproduction, representation or distribution, in whole or in part, of the content of this site without authorization is prohibited.

liability.title: Liability
liability.content: The Spark SASU strives to ensure the accuracy of the information published on the site, but cannot guarantee its completeness or timeliness. Use of the site is at the user's sole risk.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.metaDescription: Privacy policy for The Playground — data collected, purposes, legal bases, sub-processors, GDPR rights and cookies.
privacy.intro: The Playground is published by The Spark SASU (SIRET 93482575300015), with registered offices at 10 rue de la Paix, 75002 Paris, France. We are committed to protecting the privacy of our users in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). This policy describes the data we collect, the purposes of processing, your rights and how to exercise them.

controller.title: Data controller
controller.content: The data controller is The Spark SASU, represented by Dragos Dreptate.
controller.contact: For any questions regarding the protection of your personal data, you can contact us:
controller.contactMail: By mail: The Spark SASU, 10 rue de la Paix, 75002 Paris, France
controller.dpo: The Spark SASU has not appointed a Data Protection Officer (DPO) in accordance with Article 37 of the GDPR (fewer than 250 employees, no large-scale processing). The contact above fulfills this function.

identity.title: Identity and account data
identity.email: Email address (sign-in via magic link or OAuth)
identity.name: First and last name (provided during profile setup)
identity.photo: Profile picture (imported from Google/GitHub or uploaded manually)

auth.title: Authentication data
auth.oauth: OAuth account identifier (Google, GitHub) — for authentication only

payment.title: Payment data (paid events only)
payment.stripe: Payment details (card number, expiration date, CVV) are processed exclusively by Stripe and are never stored by The Playground
payment.ref: We only retain the Stripe transaction reference and receipt URL

analytics.title: Browsing and analytics data
analytics.pages: Pages visited, visit duration, navigation events
analytics.identifier: Technical identifier (pseudonymized for non-authenticated visitors, linked to account for signed-in users)
analytics.technical: Technical data: browser type, operating system

errors.title: Data collected automatically in case of errors
errors.traces: Technical error traces (stack traces) for bug diagnosis and resolution

items.authData: Email, name, OAuth identifier, profile picture
items.notifications: Sending community notifications (new Events, Host messages)
items.notificationsLegal: Legitimate interest (Art. 6.1.f) — you can unsubscribe at any time from your profile
items.analyticsLegal: Legitimate interest (Art. 6.1.f) — continuous service improvement, data hosted in EU

cookies.title: Cookies

necessary.title: Strictly necessary cookies (set without consent)
necessary.note: These cookies are essential for the website to function. They cannot be disabled.

analytics.title: Analytics cookies
analytics.posthog: Service usage analysis (pages visited, user journeys)
analytics.note: These cookies help us understand how the service is used in order to improve it. Collected data is hosted in the European Union. You can disable these cookies through your browser settings.

cookies.noAds: No advertising cookies are used by The Playground.

subprocessors.title: Sub-processors and data recipients
subprocessors.intro: The following sub-processors are involved in operating the platform. Depending on their role, they may receive personal data, technical data, or content you have entered:

vercel.data: IP address, session cookies, HTTP requests (transit)
vercel.safeguards: Vercel DPA, data hosted in EU

neon.data: All account data: email, name, profile picture, registrations, comments
neon.safeguards: Neon DPA, data hosted in EU

posthog.data: User identifier, email, name, pages visited
posthog.safeguards: PostHog DPA, data hosted in EU

sentry.data: Technical error traces (no personal data explicitly transmitted)
sentry.safeguards: Sentry DPA, data hosted in EU

resend.data: Recipient email address, name (in some emails)
resend.safeguards: Resend DPA, EU standard contractual clauses

stripe.safeguards: PCI-DSS certified, Stripe DPA, EU standard contractual clauses

anthropic.data: User-entered content (event titles and descriptions) — no personal data
anthropic.safeguards: Anthropic DPA, EU standard contractual clauses

slack.purpose: Internal administration notifications (new users, new communities)
slack.data: User name and email (admin notifications only)
slack.safeguards: Slack/Salesforce DPA, EU standard contractual clauses

subprocessors.noSale: Your data is never sold or shared with third parties for commercial or advertising purposes.

transfers.title: Transfers outside the European Union
transfers.intro: Most of our sub-processors host your data in the European Union, even when their headquarters are in the United States:
transfers.eu: Data hosted in EU: Vercel (Frankfurt), Neon (Frankfurt), PostHog (EU), Sentry (Germany)
transfers.us: Data hosted in United States: Resend (email delivery), Stripe (payment processing), Anthropic (AI features), Slack (internal notifications)
transfers.safeguards: Transfers to the United States are governed by:
transfers.scc: Standard contractual clauses adopted by the European Commission (implementing decision 2021/914)
transfers.dpa: Contractual commitments from each sub-processor (Data Processing Agreements)
transfers.conclusion: Core data (user database, application hosting, analytics, monitoring) is stored in the European Union.

rights.title: Your rights (GDPR)
rights.content: Under the GDPR, you have the following rights:

items.access: Right of access (Art. 15): obtain confirmation of data processing and receive a copy
items.rectification: Right to rectification (Art. 16): correct inaccurate or incomplete data
items.deletion: Right to erasure (Art. 17): request deletion of your data. You can delete your account directly from your profile (immediate and irreversible deletion)
items.portability: Right to data portability (Art. 20): receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format
items.opposition: Right to object (Art. 21): object to processing based on legitimate interest
items.consent: Right to withdraw consent (Art. 7.3): where processing is based on your consent, withdraw it at any time without affecting the lawfulness of processing carried out before withdrawal

rights.contactMail: By mail: The Spark SASU, 10 rue de la Paix, 75002 Paris, France
rights.delay: We commit to responding to your request within one month of receipt (Art. 12.3 GDPR).
rights.complaint: If you believe that the processing of your data constitutes a violation of the GDPR, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the French data protection authority (CNIL): www.cnil.fr.

retention.title: Data retention
retention.account: Account data (email, name, profile) — as long as the account is active
retention.usage: Usage data (registrations, comments) — as long as the account is active
retention.payment: Payment data (Stripe references) — 5 years after the transaction (legal accounting obligation)
retention.deletion: When you delete your account, your personal data is erased immediately (irreversible deletion). Data related to legal obligations (payment references) is retained in accordance with applicable legal requirements.

changes.title: Changes to this policy
changes.content: We may update this policy to reflect changes in our practices or regulations. In the event of a substantial change, we will notify you by email or by notification on the platform. The date of the last update is indicated at the top of this page.

terms.title: Terms of use
terms.metaDescription: Terms of use for The Playground — acceptance, service description, responsibilities and applicable law.
terms.intro: These terms of use ("Terms") govern the use of The Playground platform, published by The Spark SASU.

acceptance.title: Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.content: By using The Playground, you accept these Terms in their entirety. If you do not accept these conditions, please do not use the service.

service.title: Service description
service.content: The Playground is a free platform for creating communities and organizing Events. The service is accessible via the web and is provided "as is".

account.title: User account
account.content: Access to the service requires creating an account via email (magic link) or via an OAuth provider (Google, GitHub). You are responsible for the confidentiality of your account and all activity that occurs under it.

userContent.title: User content
userContent.content: You are solely responsible for content you publish on The Playground (Community descriptions, Event descriptions, comments). Any unlawful, defamatory, discriminatory or otherwise inappropriate content is prohibited and may be removed without notice.

hostResponsibilities.title: Host responsibilities
hostResponsibilities.content: As a Host, you are responsible for the accuracy of information related to your Events (location, date, description). You agree to comply with all applicable laws regarding event organization.

playerResponsibilities.title: Member responsibilities
playerResponsibilities.content: As a Member, you agree to respect the rules of each community and Event you participate in. Joining an Event constitutes a commitment to attend.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.content: The Playground is entirely free to use. No commission is charged by the platform. For paid Events, only Stripe transaction fees (approximately 2.9% + $0.30) apply and are borne by the Host.

payments.title: Payments and transactions
payments.content: The Playground acts as a technical intermediary via Stripe Connect. The platform facilitates the transaction between the Host and the Member but is not a party to the sale. Payment data is processed exclusively by Stripe and is never stored by The Playground. The refund policy is set by each Host when creating the event: either refundable (full refund on cancellation) or non-refundable. If an event is cancelled by the Host, a full refund is always issued regardless of the refund policy. In case of dispute, the Member should contact the Host. As a last resort, a chargeback can be filed with their bank.

liability.title: Limitation of liability
liability.content: The Spark SASU shall not be liable for any direct or indirect damages resulting from the use of the service, including but not limited to service interruption, data loss or disputes between Hosts and Members.

modification.title: Modification of Terms
modification.content: The Spark SASU reserves the right to modify these Terms at any time. Users will be notified of changes by email or notification on the platform. Continued use of the service after modification constitutes acceptance of the new conditions.

law.title: Applicable law
law.content: These Terms are governed by French law. In the event of a dispute, the competent courts of Paris shall have exclusive jurisdiction.

meta.title: Help · The Playground
meta.description: Everything you need to know to use The Playground — registration, community creation, event management and more.

hero.title: How does it work?
hero.subtitle: The Playground is a free platform to create and join communities. Organizers, manage your events and your network. Members, discover events near you and join communities that match your interests.

inscription.title: Join an event
inscription.intro: Most of the time, you arrive on The Playground via a shared link — via message, social media, or email. This link points directly to the event page.
inscription.step1: Read the details: date, location, description, number of attendees
inscription.step3: If you don't have an account yet, you can sign in in seconds (magic link by email or via Google / GitHub) — no form to fill out
inscription.step4: Once registered, you receive a confirmation email with the details and a link to add the event to your calendar (Google Calendar, Apple Calendar, or .ics file)
inscription.callout: No prior account is needed to view an event page. Registration takes less than a minute.
inscription.approvalNote: Some events require approval by the organizer. In that case, the button reads “Join (subject to approval)”. Your request is sent to the organizer, and you receive a confirmation email once it is accepted. If your request is declined, you are notified by email. While your request is pending, it appears in Dashboard with the status “Pending approval”.

join.title: Join a community
join.intro: When you register for an event, you automatically become a member of its community. Nothing else to do.
join.item3: See other members and visit their public profile
join.item4: Be notified of new events by email (based on your preferences)
join.outro: You can also join a community without registering for an event, from the Explore page or from a community page. On Explore, you can filter communities by topic and sort them (Recommended, Popularity, Date). The Featured section highlights a selection of active communities. If the community is private, the organizer can send you a private invite link — click it to join directly, no event registration required.

waitlist.title: Waitlist
waitlist.intro: If an event shows as full, you can join the waitlist. If a spot opens up (after a cancellation), you are automatically registered and receive a confirmation email.
waitlist.positionNote: Your position in the waitlist is shown directly on the event page (e.g. "You are on the waitlist · #3"), as an indicator displayed above the cancel button.

calendar.title: Add to calendar
calendar.intro: After registering, an "Add to calendar" button is available on the event page. Click it to choose your format:
calendar.item1: Google Calendar: opens directly in your browser
calendar.item2: .ics file: compatible with Apple Calendar, Outlook and most calendar apps

cancel.title: Cancel your registration
cancel.step1: Go to the event in question
cancel.outro: If you were on the waitlist, your spot is simply removed.
cancel.statusNote: While you are registered, a "You are registered" indicator appears on the event page, above the cancel button.

paidEvents.title: Paid events
paidEvents.intro: Some events require payment. The price is shown on the registration button (e.g. Join — 15.00 EUR).
paidEvents.step1: Click the registration button — you'll be redirected to a secure Stripe payment page
paidEvents.step2: Enter your card details and confirm
paidEvents.step3: You're automatically registered and receive a confirmation email with your payment receipt
paidEvents.refund1: Each event is either refundable (automatic refund on cancellation) or non-refundable (no refund if you cancel)
paidEvents.refund2: If the event is cancelled by the organizer, you are always refunded regardless of the policy
paidEvents.refund3: Refunds are processed within 5 to 10 business days to your card
paidEvents.callout: The Playground takes no commission. Your payment data is handled by Stripe and is never stored by the platform.

comments.title: Comments
comments.intro: Each event has a comment thread. You can ask questions, share your excitement, or chat with other attendees. Comments are visible to all registered members and the organizer.
comments.photos1: You can add up to 3 photos to each comment (JPG, PNG, WebP)
comments.photos2: Large photos are automatically compressed for fast upload, even from a phone
comments.photos3: Click a photo to enlarge it

mySpace.title: My Dashboard
mySpace.intro: Dashboard is your personal hub. You'll find there:
mySpace.item1: Your upcoming events — those you're registered for and those you organize (including drafts)
mySpace.item3: The communities you belong to or organize
mySpace.item4: Your profile (name, avatar, notification preferences) — including a link to your public profile, visible to other members
mySpace.item5: An organizer filter (visible if you organize at least one community) to show only your organized events and communities

notifications.title: Manage notifications
notifications.intro: In your profile (Dashboard → Profile), you can enable or disable certain emails. Three preferences are available:
notifications.item1: New registration for one of your events (for organizers)
notifications.item3: New event published in a community you belong to
notifications.item4: Some emails are sent automatically and cannot be disabled: registration confirmation, event reminders, event update or cancellation notifications.

leave.title: Leave a community
leave.intro: From a community page in Dashboard, click Leave community. Your registrations for upcoming events are not automatically cancelled — remember to cancel them separately if needed.
leave.memberBadge: While you are a member, a "You are a member" indicator is shown on the community page, above the Leave button.

networks.title: Networks
networks.intro: A Network groups several related communities under a shared showcase page. Each Network has a public page displaying its member communities, with their upcoming events and member count.
networks.item1: On a community page, a Member of [Network] badge appears in the info section if the community belongs to one or more networks
networks.item2: Click the network name to visit its page and discover other member communities
networks.item3: There is no network membership — you are a member of individual communities

createCommunity.title: Create your community
createCommunity.intro: Your community is your central space on The Playground. It's from here that you manage your events and your network.
createCommunity.step3: Fill in: name, description, topic, city, visibility (public or private)
createCommunity.step4: Add a cover image (optional but recommended)
createCommunity.callout: A public community appears in Explore (the platform's public directory), is accessible to everyone, and may be featured in the Featured section. A private community is only accessible via a direct link and does not appear in the directory.

createEvent.title: Create an event
createEvent.intro: From your community, click Create an event.
createEvent.field2Desc: start and end. If the event ends the next day or spans multiple days, both dates are shown on the event page
createEvent.field4Desc: context, agenda, what attendees need to know
createEvent.adv1: Capacity — maximum number of attendees (automatically activates the waitlist). To switch back to unlimited capacity, click the × next to the field.
createEvent.adv2: Price — if the event is paid, payment is handled via Stripe (attendees pay directly, The Playground takes no commission)
createEvent.callout: The event is created as a draft: it is not yet visible in Explore or open for registration. When it's ready, click Publish to make it public and notify community members. On desktop, you can also click Publish directly in the creation form to create and publish in a single step.

attachments.title: Attach documents
attachments.intro: You can attach up to 3 files (PDF, JPEG, PNG or WebP, 10 MB max each) to any event — great for sharing a presentation deck, meeting notes, venue directions or any relevant document.
attachments.item1: From the event page, click Add document or drag your files into the drop zone
attachments.item2: Documents can be added when creating the event (before publishing) or at any time from the event page
attachments.item3: Attendees can see the documents directly on the event page and download them in one click
attachments.callout: On mobile, tapping a document opens it directly in a new tab. You can delete a document at any time from the event page.

manageRegistrations.title: Manage registrations
manageRegistrations.intro: From the event dashboard, click the "Members" block to open the full list. You can access:
manageRegistrations.item1: The registrant list with names, emails and status. The waitlist is displayed separately
manageRegistrations.item2: Member profiles: click a name to view their public profile (links to their social accounts: website, LinkedIn, X, GitHub if they've added them)
manageRegistrations.item3: Remove a member from the list (contextual menu next to their name)
manageRegistrations.item4: CSV export of the full list (registered + waitlist)

approvalRegistrations.title: Approve registrations
approvalRegistrations.intro: You can enable registration approval on each event and/or each community independently, from the creation or edit settings.
approvalRegistrations.eventLabel: For an event with approval enabled:
approvalRegistrations.event1: When a Member clicks Join, their request is sent to the organizer (no automatic registration)
approvalRegistrations.event2: You receive an email for each new request
approvalRegistrations.event3: From the event dashboard, a "Pending requests" section lists all requests with Approve or Decline options
approvalRegistrations.event4: If you approve: the Member receives a confirmation email and is registered (or placed on the waitlist if the event is full)
approvalRegistrations.event5: If you decline: the Member receives a notification email
approvalRegistrations.communityLabel: For a community with approval enabled:
approvalRegistrations.community1: Membership requests (from the community page or via invite link) are subject to your approval
approvalRegistrations.community2: From the community dashboard, a "Pending requests" section lets you approve or decline each request
approvalRegistrations.callout: Approval is independent on each entity — enabling approval on a community does not automatically enable it on its events, and vice versa.

contact.title: Contact your members
contact.intro: From the event dashboard, the Invite my community button lets you send an email to all community members to invite them to the event. You can personalize the message. The same broadcast can only be resent after 24 hours.

editCancel.title: Edit or cancel an event
editCancel.item1: Edit — update information at any time. Registered attendees are notified by email if the date or location changes. For a past event, the date can no longer be changed; other details (title, description, location) remain editable.
editCancel.item2: Cancel — cancel the event. All attendees receive a cancellation email.
editCancel.callout: A cancelled event cannot be reactivated. If you simply want to reschedule, edit the date rather than cancelling.
editCancel.statusBadge: The event status is always visible in the breadcrumb of your dashboard: "Draft" (amber), "Published" (green), "Cancelled" (red) or "Past" (grey).

radar.title: The Radar — avoid scheduling conflicts
radar.intro: The Radar is available in the event creation and edit forms. Once you have filled in the title, date, and physical location, it scans in-person events (Luma, Eventbrite, Meetup) in your city during the selected week, helping you detect scheduling conflicts and pick the best time slot.
radar.keywords: Keywords are automatically detected from your title and description. You can add, remove, or edit keywords then re-run the analysis to refine results.

share.title: Share your event
share.intro: Every event has a unique URL (format the-playground.fr/m/your-event). Copy this link from the event dashboard and share it directly — via WhatsApp, Instagram, email, or newsletter. No installation or prior account is required for attendees.

members.title: Manage community members
members.intro: From your community page, you can see the full member list with their emails. Each name is clickable and links to the member's public profile. You can remove a member via the ⋮ menu next to their name.
members.outro: Members automatically join your community when they register for one of your events.

coHosts.title: Share management with co-organizers
coHosts.intro: You can delegate community management to other members by promoting them to co-organizer. A co-organizer has almost all the same powers as you, except for a few sensitive actions that stay with the owner.
coHosts.right1: Create, edit, publish and delete events
coHosts.right2: Approve or reject registrations and membership requests
coHosts.right3: Update community settings and broadcast messages to members
coHosts.right4: Receive the same notifications you do (new registrations, comments, etc.)
coHosts.limit2: Manage the Stripe Connect account (for paid events)
coHosts.limit3: Promote, demote, or remove another co-organizer
coHosts.howTo: To promote a member: on the Members page of your community, open the ⋮ menu next to their name and click Promote to co-organizer. They'll receive an email confirming their new rights, and can immediately start helping you manage the community.
coHosts.demote: To revoke a co-organizer's rights, open the same menu and click Demote to member. The person remains a member of the community but loses their management rights, and receives a plain email informing them of the change.

inviteMembers.title: Invite members
inviteMembers.intro: You can invite someone directly into your community without them needing to register for an event first. Two options are available from the Share & Invite card on your community page:
inviteMembers.step1: By email — enter up to 10 email addresses and send an invitation directly from the platform. Each recipient receives an email with a link to join the community.
inviteMembers.step2: By invite link — a private invite link is available in the Share & Invite card. Copy it and share it via WhatsApp, Slack, or any other channel. If the link has been revoked, click Generate a link to create a new one.
inviteMembers.step3: The person clicks the link, signs in, and instantly joins your community.
inviteMembers.step4: They appear immediately in the member list — you receive an email notification.
inviteMembers.callout: The invite link is revocable at any time — click Revoke to deactivate it. Members who already joined are not affected. The same link can be used by multiple people.

payments.title: Enable payments
payments.intro: You can offer paid events by enabling payments on your community via Stripe Connect.
payments.step3: You'll be redirected to Stripe to complete your profile (name, address, bank details, ID)
payments.step4: Back on The Playground, the status changes to Payments enabled
payments.priceLabel: To set a price on an event:
payments.price1: In the creation/edit form, the Ticket price section is now editable
payments.price2: Enter the price in euros — an estimated net amount (after Stripe fees) is displayed
payments.price3: Toggle Refundable on or off to set the refund policy
payments.billing1: Each paid event's dashboard shows a Ticketing summary (paid registrations, amount collected, refunded)
payments.billing2: For full details, click View my payments in the community settings — this opens the Stripe Express dashboard
payments.callout: The Playground takes no commission. Only Stripe fees (~2.9% + $0.30) are deducted from your earnings. The full remaining amount is transferred to you.

q1.question: Is The Playground free?
q1.answer: Yes, completely free for organizers and Members. The Playground takes no commission. The only applicable fees are Stripe fees (~2.9% + $0.30) on paid events, charged directly by Stripe.

q2.question: What's the difference between a public and private community?
q2.answer: A public community is visible in Explore (the public directory, filterable by topic) and accessible to everyone. A private community is only accessible via a private invite link generated by the organizer — it doesn't appear in the directory.

q3.question: Can I organize events without creating a community?
q3.answer: No. The community is the central space on The Playground. Every event belongs to a community. Creating a community only takes a few minutes.

q4.question: What happens if an event is full?
q4.answer: You can join the waitlist. If a spot opens up, you are automatically registered and notified by email.

q5.question: Do attendees need an account to register?
q5.answer: They need to create an account, but the process is very quick: an email is enough (magic link, no password), or sign in with Google / GitHub. No long forms.

q6.question: How do I access or delete my data?
q6.answer: For any request related to your data (access, export, account deletion — GDPR rights), contact us directly at hello@the-playground.fr. Account deletion is also available from Dashboard → Profile. Our privacy policy (accessible from the footer) covers all data collected, its purpose, retention periods, and your rights.

q7.question: How do I report inappropriate content?
q7.answer: Contact us directly by email at hello@the-playground.fr, describing the content in question (link to the page, description of the issue). We review every report as quickly as possible.

q8.question: How do paid events work?
q8.answer: The organizer enables payments via Stripe Connect in their community settings, then sets a price on their events. The Member pays the displayed price through a secure Stripe page. The Playground takes no commission — only Stripe fees (~2.9% + $0.30) are charged and borne by the organizer. The refund policy (refundable or non-refundable) is set by the organizer. If the event is cancelled, refunds are always automatic.

contact.title: A question?
contact.subtitle: Our team will get back to you as soon as possible.
contact.success: Message sent! We'll get back to you as soon as possible.
contact.error: An error occurred. Please try again or contact us directly at hello@the-playground.fr

Contact.pageDescription: Contact The Playground team. We reply personally within 24h.
Contact.heroDescription: A question, an idea, feedback? We read every message and reply personally within 24h.
Contact.successDescription: Thank you for your message. We will reply personally within 24h.
Contact.notePrefix: No account needed. For frequently asked questions, check our
Contact.errorRateLimited: Too many messages sent. Please try again in a few minutes.

About.pageDescription: The Playground is the free community platform for organizing events — an alternative to Meetup and Luma, with no fees or subscriptions.
